name: The Unlit Shore
description: The gallery opens onto the rim of a still, black expanse that could be water or could be a floor made of settled dark; nothing thrown into it disturbs a surface, and nothing thrown into it returns. Along the shore lie the leavings of the things that hunt this deep: cracked bones bleached to the same grey as the dust, the collapsed husk of some long-dead delver, and stranger relics carried down from the burning world above and left to cool forever in the cold. Whatever light you carry lays a single wan reflection on the black, and the reflection is always a pace behind where it should be.
ambient_messages: The black expanse laps once against the shore without sound and lies flat again.
A husk on the shore settles as something small and hungry withdraws from it into the dark.
Your reflection on the black surface lags a heartbeat behind your movement, then catches up wrong.
